game_info.title: Mount Your Friends - Physics Climbing Game
game_info.concept:
Experience the extreme simulated sporting sensation that's sweeping our digital nation! Mount Your Friends is a fiercely competitive sporting event for you to play with your friends in the contextual security of your own home, assuming that your home is not composed of sexual innuendo. Experience the extreme simulated sporting sensation that's sweeping our digital nation! Mount Your Friends is a fiercely competitive sporting event for you to play with your friends in the contextual security of your own home, assuming that your home is not composed of sexual innuendo. If it is, we may suggest buying a new home or at least getting the current one cleaned professionally.

Mount Your Friends is a physics based competitive climbing game where what you climb is each other. Players take turns climbing to the top of a tower of previous climbers (and a goat) working to become the new highest point before time runs out. If you run out of time before reaching the top, you're eliminated. It's simple to get into, but difficulty to master. Adept competitors can create massive towers by flinging themselves faster than simple climbing methods can attain. Of course, you may also end up just falling all the way down a tower if you miss!
game_info.description: Mount Your Friends is a physics-based competitive climbing game where you must climb a tower of previous climbers (and a goat base) to reach the top before time runs out. Each round, players take turns attempting to climb higher than the last successful climber. The tower gets taller and more unstable as more climbers join. If you fail to reach the top before the timer expires, you're eliminated! Master the art of grabbing, pulling, and flinging yourself upward to create massive towers and outlast your opponents.
game_info.controls:
Left Arrow: Move left / Grab left
Right Arrow: Move right / Grab right
Up Arrow: Pull yourself up when grabbing
Down Arrow: Let go / Drop down
Space: Jump / Launch yourself
Shift: Sprint (faster movement)
Z: Quick grab (grab nearest limb)
Enter: Start game
Escape: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart to menu

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic climbing mechanics and grab system. This test validates that the player can successfully grab onto body parts, pull themselves up, and navigate the tower structure without falling immediately.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The bot will attempt to climb methodically by alternating between left and right grabs, pulling up consistently, and making small jumps when necessary. It will prioritize stable grabs on the torso and avoid risky movements.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test is successful if the bot can grab onto at least 3 different body parts, pull itself up at least 2 body lengths, and maintain grip for at least 5 seconds without falling. This demonstrates that core climbing mechanics work correctly.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing optimal climbing strategy to reach maximum height. This validates the win condition, timer management, and advanced movement techniques like flinging and momentum-based climbing.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The bot will use an aggressive climbing strategy: rapidly alternating grabs, using jump launches to gain height quickly, and timing pulls to maximize upward velocity. It will aim for the highest body parts and use momentum to climb faster than the timer.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Success is reaching the top of a 4+ climber tower before the timer runs out, triggering the next round. This proves the game progression system works and that skilled play can overcome the time pressure.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing physics stability and collision detection. This ensures that climbers don't clip through each other, fall through the ground, or experience unstable physics behavior that breaks gameplay.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The bot will perform stress tests by making rapid movements, jumping into the tower from different angles, and attempting to grab multiple body parts in quick succession. It will also test edge cases like grabbing at extreme angles.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The test passes if no physics glitches occur (clipping, infinite falling, jittering), all collisions are resolved correctly, and the tower remains stable even with aggressive player movement. Frame rate should stay above 45 FPS.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing elimination system and round progression. Validates that players are correctly eliminated when time runs out, rounds advance properly, and the game correctly identifies when all players are eliminated.
TEST_4.strategy_description: The bot will intentionally fail to climb by moving slowly or in the wrong direction, allowing the timer to expire. It will test multiple rounds to ensure elimination carries across rounds correctly.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Success means the timer correctly counts down, elimination triggers when time expires, the next round starts with the remaining players, and game over occurs when all players are eliminated or only one remains.
TEST_4.description: Testing elimination system and round progression. Validates that players are correctly eliminated when time runs out, rounds advance properly, and the game correctly identifies when all players are eliminated.
TEST_4.strategy: The bot will intentionally fail to climb by moving slowly or in the wrong direction, allowing the timer to expire. It will test multiple rounds to ensure elimination carries across rounds correctly.
TEST_4.expected: Success means the timer correctly counts down, elimination triggers when time expires, the next round starts with the remaining players, and game over occurs when all players are eliminated or only one remains.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing grab release mechanics and falling recovery. This validates that players can intentionally let go, fall safely, and attempt to recover by grabbing again mid-fall.
TEST_5.strategy_description: The bot will climb partway up, release its grip, fall, and attempt to grab onto lower body parts during the fall. It will test the timing window for mid-air grabs and recovery mechanics.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: The test is successful if the bot can release grip on command, fall with proper physics, and successfully re-grab during the fall at least 50% of the time. This proves the grab system works dynamically during all movement states.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing tower instability and balance mechanics. Validates that the tower becomes progressively harder to climb as it grows taller and that physics interactions create interesting challenge.
TEST_6.strategy_description: The bot will build a tower of 5+ climbers by successfully completing multiple rounds, then attempt to climb the full height. It will test whether the tower sways, whether higher climbers are harder to reach, and whether balance matters.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Success is achieving a tower of at least 5 climbers with visible swaying/physics effects, increased difficulty in reaching the top due to height and instability, and successful completion of the challenge showing it's difficult but fair.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing rapid input handling and combo movements. Ensures the game can handle quick successive inputs without dropping commands or creating unintended behavior.
TEST_7.strategy_description: The bot will execute rapid sequences of inputs: quick grab-release cycles, jump-grab combos, directional changes while grabbing, and sprint-jump combinations. It will test input buffering and responsiveness.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: The test passes if all inputs are registered correctly within 100ms, no input sequences cause crashes or glitches, combo movements execute smoothly, and the player maintains responsive control even during rapid input changes.
